summary: Telegram Configuration Feature Testing Complete. All 11 backend API tests passed (100%). All 12 frontend UI tests passed. Telegram credentials are auto-enabled when TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID are present in .env. Settings page correctly shows Telegram Bot as 'Connected', Bot Configured as 'Yes', Trade Alerts ON, Signal Alerts ON, and Messages Sent = 1.

frontend_ui_tests: Global Analytics page loads with data-testid='global-analytics-page'
Test Alert button present and enabled

critical_code_review_comments: Auto-enable logic in server.py line 165-169 correctly calls telegram_notifier.configure(enabled=True, signals_enabled=True) when credentials are present
TelegramNotifier.configured property correctly checks both TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
POST /api/telegram/test endpoint at line 495-505 returns proper response format with success boolean and stats
Settings.jsx TelegramSection correctly displays all Telegram status indicators and toggles
Frontend properly fetches config and displays telegram.configured, telegram.enabled, telegram.signals_enabled
context_for_next_testing_agent: Telegram configuration feature fully tested. TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID are now set in backend/.env. Auto-enable logic: when credentials present, telegram starts with enabled=true and signals_enabled=true. GET /api/config returns credentials_present.telegram=true and telegram object with configured/enabled/signals_enabled/total_sent fields. POST /api/telegram/test sends test message and returns {success, stats}. Settings page shows all Telegram status in Credentials section (Telegram Bot: Connected) and Telegram Alerts section (Bot Configured: Yes, Trade Alerts: ON, Signal Alerts: ON, Messages Sent: 1, Send Test Alert button enabled).
